critical.text: I'm a doctor, not a miracle worker!
It's worse than that. He's really dead, Jim.
Please state the nature of the medical emergency.
I swear, this project is held together with spit and baling wire.
If we don't operate immediately, the patient will die.
I'm going to have to ask you to get the hell out of my sickbay.
What you're describing is medically impossible, yet here we are.
The patient is coding! Clear the area!
This isn't a patient, it's a catastrophe with a pulse.

warning.text: I'm a doctor, not an engineer!
You're not seriously going to deploy this, are you?
I don't have a magic wand. I need time and proper resources.
I would advise caution, Captain. The readings are... concerning.
In my medical opinion, this needs bed rest. A lot of it.
The patient will survive, but I wouldn't call this thriving.
Optimism is wonderful, but have you looked at these charts?
I've seen healthier-looking specimens in the morgue.
The prognosis is guarded. I'd recommend close monitoring.
I must say, I've treated Klingon targs in better shape than this.

info.text: I'm a doctor, not a DevOps engineer.
A little suffering is good for the soul.
I've noticed some minor irregularities, but nothing life-threatening.
Fascinating. From a medical perspective, of course.
I find the resilience of this organism quite remarkable!
My diagnosis: mildly irritating, but you'll live.
I've noted some areas for improvement in my medical log.
You're in fair health, but don't let that go to your head.
The patient is stable, though I'd like to run a few more scans.

allClear.text: Vital signs are stable. For now.
Optimism, Captain! It's what keeps me going.
I must say, I'm impressed. Don't let it happen again.
Well, would you look at that. A clean bill of health.
I'm not sure what you did, but keep doing it.
For once, I have nothing to complain about. Medically speaking.
The patient is in remarkable condition. Textbook, even.
This calls for a celebration! Perhaps a nice Denobulan sausage.
All systems nominal. I almost don't know what to do with myself.
Full marks. Now get out of my sickbay before something breaks.
